assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and email address.
No Intuit Developer account, workspace, app, or sandbox company exists yet.
QuickBooks Online Accounting API is the candidate family unless another product is explicitly selected.

developer_goal: Create an Intuit Developer account and reach the earliest official QuickBooks first-success API or demo outcome.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved among OAuth Playground CompanyInfo sample, language-specific demo app CompanyInfo, and Payments Submit Charge.
first_success.observable_signal: Start developing documents a quick CompanyInfo cURL after Playground tokens; Build a demo app and Payments samples define alternate terminals.

route_selection.surface: Intuit Developer Portal plus QuickBooks Online get-started docs
route_selection.rule: Include account creation. Do not invent a winner among Accounting Playground, demo-app, Payments, Desktop, or Explorer routes.

candidates.name: Developer account, Accounting-scoped app, OAuth Playground, GET CompanyInfo
candidates.reason: Start developing presents this as the quick sample API call, but signup fields and exact app-creation screens remain partly undocumented.

candidates.name: Build a demo app in .NET, Java, or PHP then CompanyInfo
candidates.reason: Official alternate first-app path with heavier implementation and language forks.

candidates.name: QuickBooks Payments Submit Charge via Playground
candidates.reason: Playground lists Submit Charge under payments scope: different product outcome from CompanyInfo.

candidates.name: QuickBooks Desktop or API Explorer-only exploration
candidates.reason: Start developing links these as separate valid entry paths with different tooling.

required_path.action: Create an Intuit Developer account with basic company information and a workspace name.

required_fields.label: Basic company information
required_fields.notes: Docs state these are requested but do not enumerate current form controls.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open App dashboard and manage apps and sandboxes.

required_path.action: Create a developer app and select the scopes required by the chosen first-success route.

required_fields.label: Scopes
required_fields.notes: Accounting versus Payments scopes change which sample calls are valid.

required_path.observable_result: Development Client ID and Client Secret are available.

required_path.action: Use the sandbox company created with the developer profile, or create an additional sandbox if required by the selected route.
required_path.observable_result: A sandbox company realm ID is available for authorization and API calls.

required_path.action: In the OAuth 2.0 Playground or selected demo app, obtain an authorization code and exchange it for access and refresh tokens for the sandbox company.
required_path.observable_result: A temporary access token and realm ID are available for API calls.

required_path.action: Send the selected sample request (CompanyInfo, Payments charge, or demo-app equivalent) using the access token.
required_path.observable_result: The selected official sample response shape is returned.

external_gates.description: User consent in the OAuth authorization flow is required before access tokens are issued for a company realm.

platform_outcomes.description: Creating a developer account provisions a sandbox QuickBooks Online company with sample data.
App creation generates Development Client ID and Client Secret.

excluded.item: Production Keys & OAuth and App Store submission
excluded.reason: Sandbox Development credentials and Playground tokens are sufficient for the documented quick sample call.

excluded.item: Reading bookkeeping primers and platform overview links before account creation
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is excluded from required developer actions.

sources.title: Create and start developing your app
Get the Client ID and Client Secret for your app
Create and test with a sandbox company
Practice authorization in the OAuth Playground

uncertainties.question: Which QuickBooks product and first-success surface is canonical: Accounting Playground CompanyInfo, demo app, Payments charge, Desktop, or Explorer?
uncertainties.impact: Scopes, tools, implementation burden, and success signals all change.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human selection of one bounded developer goal, then a fresh-account walkthrough of that route.

uncertainties.question: What exact Intuit Developer signup and current app-creation screens appear today?
uncertainties.impact: Account and app field counts remain partly unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots of developer account creation and first app wizard.
